[PDF_PAGE 81 | score=3] G OVERNOR E RANN F LOWSTONE History: Erann is the son of the former governor and the grandson of his predeces- sor. His grandfather was the first governor 7th-Level Priest and Ruler of Kayolin Strength: 13 Dexterity : 11 Constitution: 14 Intelligence: 16 Wisdom: 18 Charisma: 14 Armor Class: 3 THAC0: 14 Damage: 3-8 hit points: 46 Height: 5’1” Weight: 185 Ibs. Movement: 6 Alignment: Neutral Good appointed after the Cataclysm, since during that dire convulsion a cave-in wiped out every member of the previous ruling family. The Flowstones have universally been hailed as wise and careful rulers of Kayolin, and Erann is no exception. Unlike his father and grandfather, Erann received his training i
[PDF_PAGE 69 | score=2] tamex, or unique spouts and cups that spill the liquid metal back and forth. Klar of all ages will sit and stare for hours at these dis- plays, which are commonly located in large, public chambers in the city. CREATURES OF T HORBARDIN RAPTOR B IRDS These great birds soar over the high peaks of Kharolis, making their nests among the in accessible reaches of the Anviltops. Throughout the centuries they have devel- oped a wary respect for the dwarves. It is a feeling that the inhabitants of Thorbardin return in equal measure. Armor Class: 7 Movement: 6 (flying: 36) Hit Dice: 5 THAC0: 15 No. of Attacks: 3 (see following Damage/Attack: 1-6/1-6/1-12 Special Attacks: Nil Special Defenses: N i l Mag
[PDF_PAGE 87 | score=2] refugees. Therefore, he has refrained from informing Erann Flowstone or any other dwarf of their presence. WILDTREE With the destruction of Qualinesti by advancing dragonarmies, most of the elven population fled westward, eventually seek- ing safety on Ergoth or the Sancrist Islands. Some of those sylvan folk scattered to the four winds, however. None of the refugee groups passed through as much of an ordeal as did a small clan of wild elves, the Kagonesti. At first this small band of fewer than 200 individuals intended to hide in the forests of Qualinesti, using their superior woodsmanship to avoid detection by the marauding dragonarmies. Somehow, their secluded hiding place was detected an
[PDF_PAGE 88 | score=2] 86 FERNEN N ARROWLEAF wife was killed during the attack.) It was his suggestion that the Kagonesti conceal them- 9th-Level Elven Ranger Strength: 13 Dexterity: 19 Constitution: 12 Intelligence: 16 Wisdom: 14 Charisma: 13 Armor Class: 6 THAC0: 12 Damage: 1-8 hit points: 59 Height: 5’8” Weight: 175 Ibs. Movement: 12 Alignment: Chaotic Good selves in the woods to the north of Qua- linesti. He never imagined anything as dev- astating as the dragonarmy attack would strike into their ancient homeland. He still wonders bitterly how the enemy forces found his clan’s secluded grotto. He did formulate the plan that allowed the Kagonesti to capture the ship on the Newsea, and through his leadership has
[PDF_PAGE 11 | score=1] CHISEL L OREMASTER , S UPREME However, when he is encountered on HISTORIAN OF THE D WARVES Krynn, Chisel most commonly appears as an old dwarf, so frail that he requires a Scion sturdy crutch just to hobble along. His gar- Strength: 12 ments are poor, and his possessions few. Dexterity: 11 Constitution: 12 History: Chisel was always determined to Intelligence: 20 record the story of the Smith-folk, for he Wisdom: 20 truly believed that they, and their descen- Charisma: 10 dants the dwarves, were the true salt of the Armor Class: 7 earth on Krynn. Born as a wise and intellec- THAC0: 17 tual Smith, he was changed to a Scion in Damage: 1d10 (or spell) the first blast of magic from the Graystone
[PDF_PAGE 25 | score=1] A GATE T HORWALLEN , F IRST C HIEFTAIN OF T HORIN 9th-Level Warrior Movement: Strength: Dexterity: Alignment: Constitution: IntelIigence: Wisdom: Charisma: Armor Class: THAC0: Damage: Hit points: Height: Weight: 18/00 15 6 18 12 Neutral Good 14 15 -3 5 1d10+10 83 4'3" 200 Ibs. Special Abilities: Infravision: 60’ Direction Sense: Thorwallen had an un- canny knack for recognizing directions and distances on the surface world. It is said that he never once got lost during the course of a lifetime’s exploration of new places. Equipment: Agate typically carried a sword, an axe, and a pick (each +4 equiva- lent). His full armor included a full-face helm, plate mail, and a battered shield (+4) hat 
[PDF_PAGE 26 | score=1] 24 THE C ALNAR H ORSE chieftain and his loyal bodyguards (known as the Ten). The latter 11 horses each have The rise of the Calnar can be attributed to the maximum 40 hit points allowed for the many things. One of the most significant breed. was this powerful animal, domesticated and A Calnar horse trained as a warhorse car- bred to serve the needs of the Khalkist ries its rider into a lumbering charge. The dwarves. mount and rider do not travel as quickly as some lighter horses and their human riders, Armor Class: 7 but they can hit their targets with tremen- Movement: 12 dously crushing force. In melee, the horse Hit Dice: 5 stomps with its two forehooves (1-8 points THAC0: 15 of damage) a
[PDF_PAGE 27 | score=1] ing and building new devices, or refining officially-approved work techniques. The other important class of dwarves was the marshals. They were entrusted with the enforcement of law within the city, as well as matters of danger that required arms. The marshals formed the companies of guards that manned the sentinels, the keep, and the city. They also served to patrol the streets and establishments of Thorin itself, though trouble among the Calnar was rare.
